prerequisites: A GlassFrog account with access to your organization.
A GlassFrog API key, which you supply in the connector's required api_key field.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a GlassFrog API key. The connector's api_key secret is sent on each request in the X-Auth-Token HTTP header, as shown in the GlassFrog API documentation (curl -H 'X-Auth-Token: <api_key>' https://api.glassfrog.com/api/v3/people).

setupSteps.title: Sign in to GlassFrog
setupSteps.body: Log in to your GlassFrog account at app.glassfrog.com with a user that belongs to the organization whose data you want to sync.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: Open Profile & Settings and select the API tab, then click 'Create new v5 API key' (you can also create a v3 key if preferred). The key is shown only once on creation and is never displayed again, so copy it immediately and store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the GlassFrog API key into the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends this value in the X-Auth-Token header to authenticate against the GlassFrog API (base URL https://api.glassfrog.com/api/v3/).

sources.title: How do I get API keys? - GlassFrog Support Desk
GlassFrog API Overview - GlassFrog Support Desk
